questions.question: Determine whether the relation R = {(1,1), (2,2), (3,3), (1,2), (2,1), (2,3), (3,2)} on the set {1,2,3} is reflexive, symmetric, antisymmetric, and/or transitive. Justify your answers.
expectedPoints: Check symmetry: For every (a,b), (b,a) is present → symmetric
Check antisymmetry: (1,2) and (2,1) are both present but 1≠2 → not antisymmetric
Check transitivity: (1,2) and (2,3) are present but (1,3) is missing → not transitive

questions.question: Prove that the relation 'congruence modulo n' on the integers is an equivalence relation. Your proof should explicitly demonstrate all three required properties.
expectedPoints: Define congruence: a ≡ b mod n if n divides (a-b)
Prove reflexivity: a ≡ a mod n because n divides 0 = a-a
Prove symmetry: if a ≡ b mod n then n divides (a-b), so n divides (b-a) thus b ≡ a mod n
Prove transitivity: if a ≡ b mod n and b ≡ c mod n, then n divides both (a-b) and (b-c), so n divides their sum (a-c), thus a ≡ c mod n
Conclude that all three properties hold, so it's an equivalence relation
